back_translation: It came to pass on a Sabbath day, while He was passing through the grainfields, His disciples were picking the heads of grain, rubbing them with their hands, and eating.
Some of the Pharisees then asked, 'Why are you doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?'
Jesus answered them, 'Have you never read what David did, when he and his companions were hungry?'
'That is, David entered the house of God, took the bread of the presence and ate it and gave it to his companions — which bread is not lawful for anyone to eat, except the priests only.'
Then He said to them, 'The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.'
It came to pass on another Sabbath, He entered the synagogue and was teaching. A certain man was there whose right hand was withered.
The scribes and the Pharisees were watching Him closely, to see whether He would heal on the Sabbath, in order to find grounds to accuse Him.
But He knew their thoughts, and said to the man with the withered hand, 'Get up and stand in the middle here.' So the man got up and stood.
Then Jesus said to them, 'I ask you: on the Sabbath, is it lawful to do good or to do evil — to save a life or to destroy a life?'
He looked around at all of them, then said to the man, 'Stretch out your hand.' He did so, and his hand was restored to normal.
But they were filled with extreme rage, and discussed among themselves what they should do to Jesus.
It came to pass in those days, He went out to the mountain to pray, and He prayed to God through the whole night.
At daybreak, He called His disciples to Him, then chose twelve of them, whom He also named apostles.
That is, Simon whom He named Peter, and Andrew his younger brother, James and John, Philip and Bartholomew,
Matthew and Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Simon who was called the religious-zealot,
Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who later became the betrayer.

Then He came down with them and stood on a level place, together with a large crowd of His disciples, and a great number of people from all over the region of Judea, Jerusalem, and the coastal region of Tyre and Sidon.
They came to hear Him and to be healed of their diseases. Those who were troubled by unclean spirits were also healed.
The whole crowd tried to come and touch Him, because power was going out from Him and healing everyone.
He lifted up His eyes and looked at His disciples, and said, 'You who are poor are blessed, for the kingdom of God is yours.'
'You who hunger now are blessed, for you will be filled. You who weep now are blessed, for you will laugh.'
'You are blessed, when all people hate you, when they exclude you, insult you, and cast out your name as an evil thing, because of the Son of Man.'
'On that day, rejoice and leap with joy, because your reward is great in heaven; for their ancestors did the same thing to the prophets.'
'But woe to you who are rich, for you have already received your comfort in full.'
'Woe to you who are full now, for you will hunger. Woe to you who laugh now, for you will mourn and weep.'
'Woe to you, when all people praise you well, for their ancestors did the same thing to the false prophets.'
'But I say to you who listen: love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,'
'bless those who curse you, pray for those who mistreat you.'
'Whoever slaps you on the cheek on one side, turn the other also to him; and whoever takes your cloak, do not withhold even your inner garment.'
'Give to everyone who asks of you; and whoever takes what is yours, do not demand it back.'
'As you want people to do to you, so do to them likewise.'
'If you love only those who love you, what goodness-and-credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.'
'If you do good only to those who do good to you, what goodness-and-credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.'
'If you lend only to those from whom you hope to receive back, what goodness-and-credit is that to you? For even sinners lend to sinners, in order to receive back the same amount.'

'But you, love your enemies, do good and lend without hoping for anything in return; then your reward will be great, and you will be sons of the Most High; for He shows kindness even to the ungrateful and the wicked.'
'You — be merciful-and-compassionate, just as your Father is merciful-and-compassionate.'
'Do not judge others, then you will not be judged. Do not condemn others, then you will not be condemned. Forgive, then you will be forgiven.'

'Give, and you will receive — that is, you will receive it as a good measure, pressed down, shaken to settle, overflowing, poured into your lap. For with whatever measure you measure out, it will be measured back to you with that same measure.'
He also told them a parable: 'Can a blind man lead a blind man? Will they not both fall into a pit together?'
'A student is not greater than his teacher, but when the student has been fully trained, he will be like his teacher.'
'Why do you look at the speck in your brother's eye, but do not notice the large log in your own eye?'

'Or how can you say to your brother, <Brother, let me take the speck out of your eye>, while you yourself do not see the large log in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the large log out of your eye, then you will see clearly enough to take the speck out of your brother's eye.'
'A good tree does not bear bad fruit; likewise a bad tree does not bear good fruit.'
'For each tree is known by its own fruit. For people do not gather figs from thornbushes, nor do they gather grapes from thorn-brambles.'
'A good person brings good things out of the good treasury of his heart; an evil person brings evil things out of his evil treasury. For the mouth speaks out of what overflows in the heart.'
'Why do you call Me <Lord, Lord>, but do not do what I say?'
'Everyone who comes to Me, listens to My words, and conducts-himself-accordingly — I will show you what he is like.'

'He is like a man who built a house, who dug deep down and laid a foundation on the rock. When the flood came, the current crashed against that house, but could not make the house shake, because the house was built well.'

'But whoever hears and does not conduct-himself-accordingly is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. The current crashed against that house, and immediately the house collapsed. The collapse of that house was very great.'
